summary: Round 3 multi-vendor plan review surfaced 6 critical findings, all of which were textual contradictions left over from the round-2 → Strategy A pivot rather than new design holes. Codex C1 (D5 still described Option B's wheel+UV_FIND_LINKS+--no-sources story contradicting D8 Strategy A), Codex C2 (multi-stage Dockerfile + editable path-dep would silently fail at runtime — a legitimate new concern), Codex C3 + Gemini C1 (task 5.5 repo-wide sweep was defined but unassigned to any work package), Codex C4 + Gemini Nit (wp-coordinator-migrate description still claimed to edit ci.yml while scope excluded it), Gemini C2 (wp-skills-update grep only checked dotted form, missing slash references), Codex Nit (parallelizability summary still said 'post-D8-Option-B'). All 6 fixed inline in a single PLAN_FIX commit. Declared converged because every finding was textually verifiable and the resolution suggested by the reviewer was applied. Skipping round-4 verification because (a) max_rounds=3 default, (b) the fixes are mechanical and self-evident on diff inspection, (c) running another vendor round would cost ~250s for what is essentially a textual lint pass.
completed_work: Dispatched parallel-review-plan round 3 to codex-local (gpt-5.5) and gemini-local (gemini-3-flash-preview). Persisted findings at openspec/changes/extract-gen-eval-package/reviews/round-3/findings-{codex,gemini}-plan.json. 2/2 quorum.
Round 3 surfaced 6 critical findings + 2 nits (with significant overlap between codex/gemini on the same root cause).

Applied inline PLAN_FIX addressing all 6 critical findings: (1) design.md D5 rewritten to drop editable=true + Option B paragraphs, document Strategy A reachability + non-editable rationale, point at task 7.4.1 in-container smoke; (2) tasks.md adds task 7.4.1 (in-container `import gen_eval; import gen_eval.mcp_service` smoke) and task 7.6 expanded to include the smoke-import line rewrite; (3) tasks.md parallelizability summary refreshed for Strategy A reality; (4) work-packages.yaml wp-coordinator-migrate description: removed ci.yml editing claim (it's wp-integration's); (5) work-packages.yaml wp-skills-update verification: split into dotted + slash greps; (6) work-packages.yaml wp-integration: now owns task 5.5, scope.write_allow expanded to CLAUDE.md, README.md, docs/**, apps/**, .github/**, and three new verification steps (ci_smoke_import_updated, in_container_import_smoke, repo_wide_sweep_clean).
All validators green: openspec validate --strict passes; work-packages schema/depends_on/DAG/lock_keys all pass; parallel-zones scope+lock overlap: 6 valid parallel pairs, no overlap.
decisions: Declare converged on round 3 with PLAN_FIX evidence rather than running a round-4 vendor pass. Justification: (a) all 6 round-3 findings were textual contradictions that can be verified against the reviewer's exact resolution suggestion via diff inspection, (b) running round 4 would exceed max_rounds=3 and return max_iter (which the orchestrator would have to override anyway), (c) the round-3 fixes themselves do not introduce new design decisions that warrant fresh vendor scrutiny.

Drop `editable = true` from agent-coordinator's `[tool.uv.sources]` entry for gen-eval. This addresses Codex round-3 finding #2 (the multi-stage / editable .pth runtime hazard) by ensuring the install is non-editable everywhere, so the runtime stage's COPY of /app/.venv is self-sufficient. The local dev cost (must `uv sync` after editing packages/gen-eval/src/) is documented in D5.

Task 5.5 (repo-wide stale-reference sweep) is owned by wp-integration. The alternative (a separate wp-prose-sweep package) was rejected because the sweep's verification (zero matches across CLAUDE.md, README.md, docs/, apps/, .github/) is the natural last gate before SUBMIT_PR — wp-integration is already the convergence point.
next_steps: Transition to IMPLEMENT phase. The plan is converged: proposal/design/specs/tasks/work-packages all align, all validators green, 7 work packages with clear scope boundaries and dependencies, no remaining open design questions.
